name: Edge MCP Host
summary: Run MCP servers that must live on a user's own computer (GUI apps, real browser, local files/devices) over the mesh, transparently.
description: Some MCP servers can only run where the user actually is -- driving Adobe InDesign/Photoshop/Blender, the user's real logged-in browser, local filesystems or attached devices. Edge MCP Host runs and drives those local-only MCP servers on the user's own machine across the Tailscale mesh (no remote desktop), keeping a warm session and wrapping every call in a transparency proxy that logs args/results/latency. Register an edge host (any machine, any account; SSH key in the vault) and an MCP server via a RECIPE (plugin-app for GUI+plugin apps, browser-attach for the real browser). Driven by the `edge-mcp` CLI. Proven at scale (built a 356-page InDesign book through it).

requires.label: SSH private key to reach the edge host (ClaudeFather mints a dedicated one; the user authorizes it on their machine)

help.sub: MCP servers running on users' own computers, over the mesh
help.h: Edge MCP Host runs MCP servers that must live on a user's own machine (Adobe InDesign, their real browser, local files/devices) and drives them from here, no remote desktop. Each host is a registered machine reached by SSH (key in the vault); each server runs there via a recipe (plugin-app, browser-attach). Every tool call flows through a transparency proxy -- shown here as live activity. Drive it with the edge-mcp CLI.

launch_points.name: Edge MCP Host
launch_points.description: Register/drive local-only MCP servers on a user's machine.
